item.title: One of two Baisu escapees shot dead
item.description: Of the two prisoners still on the run after the recent Baisu Correctional Institution breakout, one has been shot dead during a dawn raid by a joint Police and Correctional Service operation, leaving only one escapee still at large. Western Highlands Provincial Police Commander Chief Superintendent John Sagom confirmed it saying the joint operation is another breakthrough in the manhunt that began
item.content:encoded:
Of the two prisoners still on the run after the recent Baisu Correctional Institution breakout, one has been shot dead during a dawn raid by a joint Police and Correctional Service operation, leaving only one escapee still at large.

Western Highlands Provincial Police Commander Chief Superintendent John Sagom confirmed it saying the joint operation is another breakthrough in the manhunt that began after 10 National Court remandees escaped from Baisu Correctional Institution earlier this month.

"The joint operation by Police and the Correctional Service has been very successful. Of the 10 prisoners who escaped, nine have now been accounted for, and only one remains outstanding," Chief Superintendent Sagom said.

He said the operation is continuing, with police and Correctional Service officers working together to track down the final escapee.

Chief Superintendent Sagom has called on the remaining fugitive to surrender immediately to the nearest police station or seek the help of a trusted person to notify police.

"I want to tell the last escapee who is still on the run to surrender immediately. It is in your own best interest. If you cannot come directly to police, pass a message through someone you trust so police can pick you up peacefully," he said.

The Provincial Police Commander also warned the public against hiding or helping the remaining escapee.

"The law works both ways. It applies to the escapee and also to anyone who is harbouring him. Police will not hesitate to arrest and charge anyone found assisting this suspect," Chief Superintendent Sagom warned.

Sagom also revealed that one person has already been apprehended for allegedly harboring an escapee (the recent one shot dead) and investigators are preparing the appropriate charges.

Chief Superintendent Sagom added police are confident the final escapee will soon be captured as the joint operation continues.

item.title: PNG Power selling electricity at a loss: Duma
item.description: PNG Power is selling electricity for less than it costs to buy it, outgoing minister William Duma says, warning that the pricing system has left the state-owned company struggling financially. Speaking during the handover of PNG Power responsibilities to the new minister, Richard Maru on Monday, Mr Duma said PNG Power sells electricity at an average of 87 toea per kilowatt hour but buys it for mor
item.content:encoded:
PNG Power is selling electricity for less than it costs to buy it, outgoing minister William Duma says, warning that the pricing system has left the state-owned company struggling financially.

Speaking during the handover of PNG Power responsibilities to the new minister, Richard Maru on Monday, Mr Duma said PNG Power sells electricity at an average of 87 toea per kilowatt hour but buys it for more than K1 per kilowatt hour.

"PNG Power sells power at about 87 toea per kilowatt hour, but buys it at more than one kina per kilowatt hour," Mr Duma said.

"So where is the logic? When you have that tariff, this company will continue to lose money."

Mr Duma said the company has faced this challenge for many years and has continued to record financial losses.

He said long-term agreements with independent power producers have also increased the cost of supplying electricity.

"As long as these agreements remain in force, PNG Power will never be able to make a profit unless something changes," he said.

Despite the challenges, Mr Duma said PNG Power is beginning to recover through reforms introduced by the government and the company's management.

He said PNG Power recorded a profit of K1.7 million last year after more than 20 years of losses.

"That is a good turnaround. It is a sign of better things to come," he said.

Mr Duma said the government will continue working to reduce costs and improve the company's financial performance.

item.title: State owes PNG Power nearly K200 Million
item.description: Outgoing Minister responsible for PNG Power, William Duma, says unpaid electricity bills by government agencies are one of the biggest reasons the state-owned power company continues to face financial difficulties. Speaking during the handover of PNG Power responsibilities to the new minister, Richard Maru on Monday, Mr Duma said government departments, hospitals, schools, police stations and othe
item.content:encoded:
Outgoing Minister responsible for PNG Power, William Duma, says unpaid electricity bills by government agencies are one of the biggest reasons the state-owned power company continues to face financial difficulties.

Speaking during the handover of PNG Power responsibilities to the new minister, Richard Maru on Monday, Mr Duma said government departments, hospitals, schools, police stations and other state agencies owe PNG Power almost K200 million.

"Our state agencies owe this company nearly K200 million," Mr Duma said.

"When your single biggest customer does not pay its bills, how do you expect the company to make a profit, pay dividends or improve its ageing infrastructure?" he asked.

Mr Duma said PNG Power has continued to struggle financially despite efforts by the government and the company's management to improve its performance.

He said the company also faces high costs from long-term agreements with independent power producers, where PNG Power must continue paying for electricity under existing contracts.

"As long as these agreements remain in force, PNG Power will never be able to make a profit unless something changes," he said.

Despite these challenges, Mr Duma said PNG Power is beginning to recover under the leadership of Chief Executive Officer Paul Bailey.

He said the company recorded a small profit of K1.7 million last year after more than two decades of losses.

"That is a good turnaround. It is a sign of better things to come," Mr Duma said.

He said the government will continue with reforms aimed at improving PNG Power's financial position and delivering reliable and affordable electricity to Papua New Guineans.

item.title: ICT Minister: No Papua New Guinean should be left behind
item.description: New Information and Communication Technology Minister Robert Naguri says every Papua New Guinean should have equal access to digital services, regardless of where they live. Speaking at the opening of the Digital Transformation Summit 2026 in Port Moresby on Monday, Mr Naguri said digital transformation must benefit everyone, including people in rural communities, women, youth, people living with
item.content:encoded:
New Information and Communication Technology Minister Robert Naguri says every Papua New Guinean should have equal access to digital services, regardless of where they live.

Speaking at the opening of the Digital Transformation Summit 2026 in Port Moresby on Monday, Mr Naguri said digital transformation must benefit everyone, including people in rural communities, women, youth, people living with disabilities and small businesses.

"Digital transformation must benefit every Papua New Guinean. No one should be excluded because of geography, gender, disability, age or income," Mr Naguri said.

The minister said building trust, improving digital infrastructure, supporting innovation and promoting inclusion are the four priorities that will guide Papua New Guinea's digital transformation.

He said people will only use digital government services if they trust that their personal information is protected and that online systems are secure.

"Trust is the foundation upon which a successful society is built," he said.

Mr Naguri also said the government remains committed to expanding digital infrastructure so more provinces, districts and communities can connect to the digital economy.

He said reliable internet, secure data centres and stronger government digital systems are now essential for improving education, healthcare, business and public services.

"These are no longer optional investments. They are essential infrastructure," he said.

Mr Naguri encouraged government agencies, businesses, development partners and young innovators to work together to turn ideas from the summit into practical solutions that improve the lives of Papua New Guineans.

item.title: Drifting fishermen safe on Tanga, NIP
item.description: Authorities in Bougainville are now working to repatriate two fishermen who drifted to Tanga Island in New Ireland Province, after their boat developed engine problems during a tuna fishing trip. ABG Community Government Secretary, Belinda Maree-Gara Lanbong, confirmed her office is coordinating with relevant agencies to bring the duo safely back to Buka.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities in Bougainville are now working to repatriate two fishermen who drifted to Tanga Island in New Ireland Province, after their boat developed engine problems during a tuna fishing trip.

ABG Community Government Secretary, Belinda Maree-Gara Lanbong, confirmed her office is coordinating with relevant agencies to bring the duo safely back to Buka.

The two fishermen, Sekei Musen and Male Gaden, from Lontis in Haku Constituency, survived three days adrift before coming ashore at Sungkin Village on Tanga.

Residents of Boeng Island provided them with food, shelter and assistance.

Reports from Tanga indicate both men are in good health and awaiting arrangements for their return.

Secretary Lanbong also issued a strong warning to the public against sea travel while strong winds continue to affect Bougainville waters.

Travellers are urged to notify the Bougainville Disaster and Emergency Office before departure and report back after completing their journey so authorities can monitor their safety.

item.title: Prime Minister Marape welcomes restoration of direct Air Niugini flights to Japan
item.description: Prime Minister James Marape described the successful restoration of Air Niugini’s direct flights between Port Moresby and Narita International Airport in Japan, as a major milestone in PNG’s Aviation Sector. He also welcomed this restoration as a significant step forward in strengthening the country’s economic, tourism, investment and people-to-people ties with one of its closest and longest-stand
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister James Marape described the successful restoration of Air Niugini’s direct flights between Port Moresby and Narita International Airport in Japan, as a major milestone in PNG’s Aviation Sector.

He also welcomed this restoration as a significant step forward in strengthening the country’s economic, tourism, investment and people-to-people ties with one of its closest and longest-standing development partners.

The inaugural restored service departed Jacksons International Airport last week as Flight PX54 and landed safely at Narita International Airport after approximately six hours, marking the return of direct air connectivity between Papua New Guinea and Japan after several years.

Prime Minister Marape congratulated the staff of Air Niugini and all stakeholders whose commitment made the restoration of the important international route possible.

The Prime Minister noted that Japan has remained one of Papua New Guinea’s most trusted development and economic partners for decades, contributing significantly to infrastructure development, fisheries, energy, agriculture, health, education and human resource development.

“Japan has stood beside Papua New Guinea throughout our development journey.

“Modern aviation is one of the engines of economic growth. Every new international route creates opportunities for investment, employment, exports, tourism and commerce. Improved air connectivity reduces barriers to business and positions Papua New Guinea more competitively within the Asia-Pacific region.” PM Marape said.

Prime Minister Marape said the restoration of the Narita service aligns with the Government’s long-term vision of expanding Papua New Guinea’s international aviation network.

“We are also exploring new opportunities with nations such as the United Arab Emirates and elsewhere to establish additional direct international connections that will strengthen Papua New Guinea’s place in the global economy.

“The expansion of Jacksons International Airport demonstrates our confidence that Papua New Guinea will become an increasingly important Aviation Hub for the Pacific Region.” Mr. Marape said.

He encouraged Papua New Guineans to take advantage of the renewed service to strengthen business relationships, educational opportunities and tourism exchanges with Japan while inviting more Japanese travelers to discover Papua New Guinea.

item.title: NMSA warns boat owners to follow sea safety laws
item.description: The National Maritime Safety Authority [NMSA] will not hesitate to arrest and prosecute small boat owners and operators who do not comply to the Small Craft Act (SCA) 2011. NMSA Chief Executive Officer, Pawa Limu issued this warning in light of the recent boat incidents in East New Britain and New Ireland provinces and said, small boat owners, operators and passengers in the maritime provinces mus
item.content:encoded:
The National Maritime Safety Authority [NMSA] will not hesitate to arrest and prosecute small boat owners and operators who do not comply to the Small Craft Act (SCA) 2011.

NMSA Chief Executive Officer, Pawa Limu issued this warning in light of the recent boat incidents in East New Britain and New Ireland provinces and said, small boat owners, operators and passengers in the maritime provinces must exercise responsibility and comply with sea safety measures.

Communities in the 15 maritime communities who use the sea for their daily sustenance have been warned to check local weather report, inform others of where you are going, always wear life jacket, ensure to take extra food and drinking water, make sure the outboard motor is working properly, not to get on boat with drunkards and overload passengers.

The NMSA reminded provincial governments of the 15 maritime provinces to ensure a high-level maritime safety is enforced on people who rely on the sea for livelihood.

Mr Limu said the maritime provinces must take full responsibility to ensure SCA 2011 is effectively implemented to improve sea safety for owners and operators.

“Under the law for practical implementation and enforcement, each maritime province has different circumstances and challenges that require local solutions,” Limu said.

Mr Limu warned all boat owners, operators and passengers that anyone caught breaching the Small Craft Act will face the consequences of the law.

The NMSA Maritime Rescue Coordinating Centre [MRCC] is on standby to assist all distresses in any emergency.

MRCC can be contacted on toll free number 73517017 or email address mrcc@nmsa.gov.pg

item.title: Strong wind forces search to be stopped
item.description: The search for five missing public servants and their family members was suspended at 4pm today due to strong winds and rough sea conditions. Chairman of the Provincial Disaster Committee, Levi Mano, said sea searches had already been suspended yesterday, while the aerial search that began this morning was also called off because of the weather.
item.content:encoded:
The search for five missing public servants and their family members was suspended at 4pm today due to strong winds and rough sea conditions.

Chairman of the Provincial Disaster Committee, Levi Mano, said sea searches had already been suspended yesterday, while the aerial search that began this morning was also called off because of the weather.

Mr Mano said the aerial search team located one of the two missing boats off the coast of Messi, a coastal village in Namatanai, Central New Ireland. However, there was no sign of the passengers.

item.title: New education minister sets priorities
item.description: The new Minister for Education and Member for Kerowagi, Francis Kikin Siune, has thanked Prime Minister James Marape for his new appointment and has promised to deliver on the country's shared vision.

item.title: Joseph Manu arrives in PNG for Chiefs visit
item.description: Former Sydney Rooster and New Zealand Kiwi International Joseph Manu arrived in Port Moresby this afternoon with his family, accompanied by PNG Chiefs General Manager for Football Michael Chammas and CEO Lorna McPherson.
